data.symptoms: Conjunctivitis with discharge within first month of life
data.treatment_drugs: Tetracycline eye ointment 1% (prophylaxis); Kanamycin + Erythromycin; Ceftriaxone

data.symptoms: Bleeding tendency
data.treatment_drugs: Vitamin K IM

data.symptoms: Poor breathing, especially if mother given pethidine in labour
data.treatment_drugs: Naloxone neonatal; Adrenaline (dilute 1:10,000); Sodium bicarbonate slow IV

data.symptoms: Few localising signs, lethargy, poor feeding, respiratory distress, seizures, temperature instability, feed intolerance
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin (first 48hrs); Gentamicin + Cloxacillin (after 48hrs); Ampicillin if benzylpenicillin unavailable

data.symptoms: Signs of sepsis + neurological involvement
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin + Chloramphenicol; Ceftriaxone as alternative

data.symptoms: Feed intolerance, abdominal distension, vomiting, bloody stools, perforation
data.treatment_drugs: Nil by mouth; supportive care; Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin + Metronidazole IV

data.symptoms: Muscle spasms, rigidity, difficulty feeding
data.treatment_drugs: Minimal handling; Benzylpenicillin or Procaine penicillin; Anti-tetanus immunoglobulin; Diazepam or Chlorpromazine + Phenobarbitone for spasms

data.symptoms: Signs of syphilis in neonate (rash, hepatosplenomegaly, snuffles, etc.)
data.treatment_drugs: Procaine penicillin or Erythromycin (if penicillin allergy)

data.symptoms: Yellow discolouration of skin/sclera within 24 hours of birth; visible jaundice
data.treatment_drugs: Phototherapy; Exchange transfusion; extra fluids; encourage breastfeeding

data.symptoms: Seizures, hypoglycaemia (<2.2mmol/l), hypoxic-ischaemic encephalopathy, meningitis, hypocalcaemia
data.treatment_drugs: Dextrose 50% slow IV (if hypoglycaemic); Dextrose 10% infusion; Phenobarbitone IV (loading then maintenance); Phenytoin IV; Lorazepam or Midazolam (if refractory)

data.symptoms: Dextrose <2.2mmol/l, convulsions, lethargy
data.treatment_drugs: Dextrose 50% IV (1ml/kg diluted 1:1); Dextrose 10% infusion; breast milk via NG tube

data.symptoms: Respiratory distress in premature infant due to surfactant deficiency
data.treatment_drugs: Minimal handling; Supplemental oxygen; Surfactant (100mg/kg, up to 4 doses); CPAP; IV fluids; antibiotics (for suspected sepsis)

data.symptoms: Cessation of breathing in premature infant (<32/40 weeks)
data.treatment_drugs: Caffeine citrate IV (loading then maintenance); Aminophylline (loading then maintenance)

data.symptoms: Fast breathing (age-dependent), chest indrawing, grunting, stridor, cough
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin (severe); Amoxicillin oral (first line); Procaine penicillin IM (alternative)

data.symptoms: Any general danger sign (unable to drink, vomiting everything, convulsions, lethargic/unconscious) + chest indrawing or stridor
data.treatment_drugs: First dose benzylpenicillin + gentamicin; treat hypoglycaemia; keep warm; refer URGENTLY

data.symptoms: Cough, nasal discharge, no fast breathing, no chest indrawing
data.treatment_drugs: Home care; NO antibiotics; NO antihistamines or cough mixtures; paracetamol for fever

data.symptoms: Wheeze; infant <2 months = serious illness; child >1 year may be asthma
data.treatment_drugs: Salbutamol nebulised or oral; Adrenaline SC (if no nebuliser); Prednisolone oral; refer if first episode or distress

data.symptoms: Stridor at rest, chest indrawing, barking cough, fever
data.treatment_drugs: Refer; if delay: Chloramphenicol or Ceftriaxone + Cloxacillin

data.symptoms: Very ill, toxic, drooling saliva, stridor, high fever
data.treatment_drugs: Refer URGENTLY; DO NOT examine throat; antibiotics as for croup

data.symptoms: Sudden choking, local wheeze, decreased air entry (age 1-2 years), may cause stridor/cough
data.treatment_drugs: Bronchoscopy to remove; antibiotics if secondary infection; consult cardiothoracic surgeons

data.symptoms: Fever, neck stiffness, dysphagia, stridor, drooling
data.treatment_drugs: Surgical drainage; Cloxacillin + Gentamicin

data.symptoms: Pus in lung/pleura, fever, cough, respiratory distress
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin + Gentamicin; chest drain for empyema

data.symptoms: Sore throat, pseudomembrane, "bull neck", fever, toxaemia
data.treatment_drugs: Antitoxin; Benzylpenicillin IM

data.symptoms: Paroxysmal cough with inspiratory whoop, post-tussive vomiting, fever
data.treatment_drugs: Erythromycin

data.symptoms: Ear pain, pus draining <14 days, fever, irritability
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin; Paracetamol; dry ear by wicking

data.disease: Chronic ear infection (suppurative otitis media - child)
data.symptoms: Pus draining ≥14 days, hearing loss
data.treatment_drugs: Dry wicking; Quinolone drops (ciprofloxacin/norfloxacin/ofloxacin); refer if not improving

data.symptoms: Tender swelling behind ear, fever, anterior displacement of auricle
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin; Paracetamol; refer

data.symptoms: Tender enlarged neck lymph nodes, white exudate on tonsils, fever
data.treatment_drugs: Procaine penicillin then Penicillin V; or Amoxicillin; Paracetamol

data.symptoms: Whitish patches or reddening of oral mucosa
data.treatment_drugs: Miconazole 2% gel

data.symptoms: Loose/watery stools, dehydration (sunken eyes, poor drinking, slow skin pinch, lethargic/unconscious)
data.treatment_drugs: ORS (Plan A/B/C); Zinc sulphate; Continue feeding; Ciprofloxacin or Nalidixic acid (for dysentery); Metronidazole (for amoebic dysentery)

data.symptoms: Diarrhoea lasting >14 days, may be with or without dehydration
data.treatment_drugs: Refer; feeding advice (low lactose, fermented porridge); Vitamin A; Zinc

data.symptoms: Blood in stool, cramps, fever
data.treatment_drugs: Ciprofloxacin or Nalidixic acid

data.symptoms: Rice-watery diarrhoea, severe dehydration, vomiting
data.treatment_drugs: Rehydration (IV/ORS); Ciprofloxacin or Azithromycin

data.symptoms: Wasting, bilateral pitting oedema, feeble suckling, hypothermia, hypoglycaemia, apathetic/lethargic
data.treatment_drugs: F-75 then F-100 or RUTF; Ampicillin + Gentamicin; Amoxicillin (if no complications); Vitamin A; ReSoMal; Albendazole (on discharge)

data.symptoms: Pallor, fatigue, low Hb
data.treatment_drugs: Albendazole (if hookworm); Ferrous sulphate

data.symptoms: Severe/recurrent pneumonia, generalised lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly, failure to thrive, oral candidiasis, finger clubbing
data.treatment_drugs: Cotrimoxazole prophylaxis; ART (per national guidelines, DTG-based for ≥20kg); management of OIs

data.symptoms: Respiratory symptoms in HIV+ child after first year of life
data.treatment_drugs: Short term steroids; antibiotics; refer; initiate ART

data.symptoms: Fever, chills, headache; confirmed by RDT or blood slide
data.treatment_drugs: Artemether-lumefantrine (Co-artemether) - 6-dose course over 3 days

data.symptoms: Fever, confirmed malaria
data.treatment_drugs: Co-artemether (dispersible tablet dissolved in water)

data.symptoms: Confirmed malaria in low-transmission area
data.treatment_drugs: Artemether-lumefantrine + single low-dose Primaquine (for gametocyte clearance, if G6PD normal, >1yr, >10kg, not pregnant)

data.symptoms: Prostration, altered consciousness, convulsions, hyperpyrexia, jaundice, severe anaemia (Hb≤6g/dl), hyperparasitaemia (>5%), acute renal failure, respiratory distress, hypoglycaemia, shock, haemoglobinuria, bleeding tendencies
data.treatment_drugs: IV Artesunate (minimum 3 doses) then oral Co-artemether; IV Quinine (if artesunate unavailable); supportive care (oxygen, dextrose, diazepam for convulsions, transfusion)

data.symptoms: Fever, confirmed malaria
data.treatment_drugs: Quinine + Clindamycin (7 days)

data.symptoms: Fever, confirmed malaria
data.treatment_drugs: Artemether-lumefantrine

data.symptoms: Severe malaria symptoms in pregnant woman
data.treatment_drugs: IV Artesunate (same as non-pregnant)

data.symptoms: No response after 48hrs, or recurrence of fever + parasitaemia 7-14 days after initial treatment
data.treatment_drugs: Artesunate/Amodiaquine (3 days) OR Quinine + Doxycycline or Clindamycin (7 days)

data.symptoms: Cough ≥1 week (or any duration if PLHIV), night sweats, fever, weight loss, BMI <17kg/m2, failure to thrive (children)
data.treatment_drugs: 2HRZE / 4HR (FDC) (6 months total); 2HRZE/10HR for TB meningitis, bone, joint, pericardium, disseminated, spinal disease

data.symptoms: Lymph node TB (peripheral, isolated) OR uncomplicated PTB (non-cavitary, <1 lobe, no miliary) OR uncomplicated pleural effusion
data.treatment_drugs: 2HRZE / 2HR (4 months total)

data.symptoms: All previously treated cases of any form of TB (now same as drug-sensitive regimen)
data.treatment_drugs: 2HRZE/4HR (same as new cases – no separate Category II)

data.symptoms: Resistance to rifampicin (Xpert MTB/Rif positive), may have risk factors (treatment failure, contact with DRTB, HIV, residence in high burden zone)
data.treatment_drugs: 6-month BPaLM regimen (Bedaquiline, Pretomanid, Linezolid, Moxifloxacin)

data.symptoms: Meningitis with TB aetiology
data.treatment_drugs: Intensive regimen of 6 months HRZ(Eto) with high-dose HR (specialist use)

data.symptoms: 1-5 skin lesions with loss of sensation, asymmetrical, one nerve trunk involved
data.treatment_drugs: Dapsone + Clofazimine + Rifampicin (monthly) for 6 months

data.symptoms: >5 lesions, symmetrical, many nerve trunks involved, or positive skin smear
data.treatment_drugs: Dapsone + Clofazimine (daily + monthly) + Rifampicin (monthly) for 12 months

data.symptoms: Swollen, red, tender skin lesions; nerve swelling/tenderness, new paralysis
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin; Prednisolone (if severe or neuritis)

data.symptoms: Tender subcutaneous nodules, fever, lymphadenitis, orchitis, arthritis, neuritis, ulcerating lesions
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin; Prednisolone (severe); Clofazimine (for recurrent)

data.symptoms: Itching → papule → vesicle → depressed black eschar in 2-6 days
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin then Procaine penicillin (severe); Doxycycline (less severe)

data.symptoms: Severe respiratory illness, blood-stained sputum, chest pain, dyspnoea
data.treatment_drugs: Refer to designated Infectious Disease Hospital

data.symptoms: Sudden headache, chills, prostration, fever, general pains; maculopapular rash day 5-7 (spares face/palms/soles); chancre at bite site
data.treatment_drugs: Doxycycline

data.symptoms: Bite from suspect animal (categories II and III exposure)
data.treatment_drugs: Thorough wound cleaning (povidone-iodine/soap); Rabies vaccine (ID or IM schedule); Human Rabies Immunoglobulin (RIG) for category III

data.symptoms: Visible haematuria, positive urine dipstick for blood/protein (in children/adolescents at primary care)
data.treatment_drugs: Praziquantel 40mg/kg single dose

data.symptoms: Unexplained iron deficiency anaemia, hepatosplenomegaly, non-resolving chronic salmonella infections; dysentery-like symptoms
data.treatment_drugs: Praziquantel 40mg/kg once daily, repeat at 4 weeks

data.symptoms: Fever, acute serum sickness, severe immunological reaction to recent heavy schistosoma infection
data.treatment_drugs: Praziquantel + Prednisolone

data.symptoms: Abdominal pain, anal itching (pinworm), anaemia (hookworm)
data.treatment_drugs: Albendazole single dose

data.symptoms: Abdominal symptoms, possible visible segments in stool
data.treatment_drugs: Albendazole once daily for 3 days; repeat course if not cured after 3 weeks

data.symptoms: Serpiginous, itchy, raised skin tracks (creeping eruption)
data.treatment_drugs: Albendazole once daily for 7 days

data.symptoms: Focal seizures without fever, typical CT scan appearance, may have drowsiness or focal signs
data.treatment_drugs: Praziquantel + Prednisolone (specialist inpatient treatment)

data.symptoms: Cysts in liver/lungs (serological confirmation required)
data.treatment_drugs: Albendazole (cycled 30 days on/15 days off x 4 cycles); Surgery is treatment of choice

data.symptoms: Hydrocoele, lymphoedema, elephantiasis, chyluria; acute: fever, lymphangitis, epididymo-orchitis
data.treatment_drugs: Diethylcarbamazine (DEC) + Albendazole (single dose); specialist management for chronic stage

data.symptoms: Rapid onset fever, chills, severe headache, prostration, extremely painful swollen lymph nodes; or pneumonic: cough with blood-stained sputum, chest pain, dyspnoea
data.treatment_drugs: Streptomycin or Chloramphenicol

data.symptoms: Acute haemolymphatic: fever, headache, joint pains, pruritis, eschar, lymphadenopathy. Neurological: sleep disturbance, confusion, behavioural changes
data.treatment_drugs: Fexinidazole (for both stages, age ≥6, weight ≥20kg); Suramin (first stage, <6y or <20kg); Melarsoprol (second stage, <6y or <20kg)

data.symptoms: Gradual onset of persistently high fever, chills, malaise, headache, sore throat, cough, abdominal pain, constipation or diarrhoea
data.treatment_drugs: Ciprofloxacin or Norfloxacin

data.symptoms: Similar symptoms, not responding to first-line
data.treatment_drugs: Azithromycin or Cefixime

data.symptoms: Severe illness with complications (intestinal haemorrhage, perforation)
data.treatment_drugs: Ciprofloxacin or Norfloxacin

data.symptoms: Severe illness, not responding
data.treatment_drugs: Azithromycin or Cefixime or Ceftriaxone IV

data.symptoms: Asymptomatic, positive stool/rectal culture >1 year after recovery
data.treatment_drugs: Ciprofloxacin (4 weeks); Cholecystectomy if lithiasis; treat schistosomiasis if present

data.symptoms: Urethral discharge, dysuria
data.treatment_drugs: Ceftriaxone 1g IM (single dose) + Doxycycline or Azithromycin

data.symptoms: Symptoms persist 7 days after treatment (exclude reinfection/non-compliance)
data.treatment_drugs: Cefixime or Ceftriaxone + Azithromycin + Metronidazole; consider Mycoplasma genitalium

data.symptoms: Curd-like discharge, vulval redness and itching
data.treatment_drugs: Miconazole or Clotrimazole or Nystatin pessary/cream

data.symptoms: Abnormal discharge not consistent with candida (fishy odour, frothy)
data.treatment_drugs: Metronidazole or Tinidazole

data.symptoms: Abnormal discharge + partner has urethral discharge OR mucopurulent cervicitis / easy bleeding on examination
data.treatment_drugs: Ceftriaxone IM + Doxycycline or Azithromycin (added to BV/trichomonas treatment)

data.symptoms: Anorectal pain, itching, discharge, bleeding, rectal fullness, tenesmus, constipation, mucus in stool; often asymptomatic
data.treatment_drugs: Treat according to suspected pathogen: Ceftriaxone + Doxycycline for gonorrhoea/chlamydia; Acyclovir for HSV; Benzathine penicillin for syphilis

data.disease: Genital ulcer disease (including herpes, syphilis, chancroid)
data.symptoms: Genital ulcers, may have buboes; recurrent vesicular lesions (herpes)
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin (single dose) + Ciprofloxacin + Acyclovir (10 days for primary herpes)

data.symptoms: Granulating ulcers without buboes
data.treatment_drugs: Azithromycin or Doxycycline or Erythromycin

data.symptoms: Buboes without ulcers (penile/vulval lymphoedema, inguinal buboes); may have anal discharge
data.treatment_drugs: Doxycycline or Erythromycin

data.symptoms: Acute scrotal swelling, pain (exclude torsion, hernia)
data.treatment_drugs: Ceftriaxone IM + Doxycycline or Erythromycin

data.symptoms: Chancre, rash, mucous patches, condylomata lata, asymptomatic latent
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin (single dose) or Doxycycline/Erythromycin

data.symptoms: Asymptomatic latent, CVS changes, gummas
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin weekly x 3 doses or Doxycycline/Erythromycin

data.symptoms: Neurological symptoms (tabes dorsalis, general paresis, etc.)
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin IV or Procaine penicillin or Doxycycline or Erythromycin + Prednisolone

data.symptoms: Positive serology in pregnant woman
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin (3 doses weekly); treat partner

data.disease: Congenital syphilis (infant born to treated mother)
data.symptoms: Infant born to mother with syphilis (even if mother treated)
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin single dose

data.symptoms: External, perianal wart-like growths
data.treatment_drugs: Podophyllotoxin 0.5% (self-applied) or Podophyllin paint (clinic-applied) or Imiquimod cream or Trichloroacetic acid

data.symptoms: Internal warts
data.treatment_drugs: Cryotherapy, electro-cautery, or surgical excision (NO podophyllin)

data.symptoms: Small, dome-shaped, umbilicated papules
data.treatment_drugs: Lesion pricking/expression; cryotherapy; (if extensive, suspect HIV and refer)

data.symptoms: Itching in pubic area, visible lice/nits
data.treatment_drugs: Permethrin 5% cream or Benzyl benzoate 25% emulsion or Gamma benzene hexachloride 1% lotion

data.symptoms: Lymph nodes >1.5cm in ≥2 areas, persisting ≥1 month, no other cause
data.treatment_drugs: No specific treatment; exclude TB, Kaposi's, lymphoma, syphilis; ART

data.symptoms: Whitish patches, reddening, angular cheilitis
data.treatment_drugs: Nystatin suspension/lozenges or Miconazole oral gel

data.symptoms: Odynophagia (pain on swallowing), chest pain, difficulty swallowing
data.treatment_drugs: Fluconazole (systemic); ART referral; cotrimoxazole prophylaxis

data.symptoms: ≥3 liquid stools daily for 2-14 days
data.treatment_drugs: Rehydration; Ciprofloxacin + Ceftriaxone (if severe/toxic); Ciprofloxacin (if bloody)

data.symptoms: ≥3 liquid stools daily for >1 month, continuous or episodic
data.treatment_drugs: Rehydration, nutrition; Loperamide or Codeine (if disabling); Ciprofloxacin + Ceftriaxone if fever/bloody; refer if weight loss

data.symptoms: Weight loss >10% + unexplained chronic diarrhoea >1 month OR unexplained prolonged fever >1 month
data.treatment_drugs: High calorie/protein diet; Nicotinamide + Pyridoxine + Thiamine; treat underlying conditions (especially TB); ART

data.symptoms: WHO stage 3/4 or CD4 ≤200 cells/mm³ (age ≥5); all children <5 years
data.treatment_drugs: Screen for TB (symptom screen, CXR, LF-LAM), cryptococcal antigen (if CD4<200), malnutrition; Treat OIs; Optimize ART; Prevent with cotrimoxazole, TPT, fluconazole pre-emptive therapy, vaccines

data.symptoms: Progressive shortness of breath (exertion early), cyanosis, dry cough, few chest signs, bilateral interstitial infiltrates on CXR
data.treatment_drugs: High-dose Cotrimoxazole (21 days); Prednisolone (if hypoxic/tachypnoeic); Clindamycin + Primaquine (sulpha allergy)

data.symptoms: Subacute headache, fever, neck stiffness, photophobia, confusion, nausea/vomiting, visual/hearing changes, seizures, focal neurology (raised ICP)
data.treatment_drugs: Liposomal Amphotericin B single dose + Flucytosine + Fluconazole (2 weeks induction); then Fluconazole (8 weeks consolidation); then Fluconazole maintenance (until CD4>100 and VL<1000 for 6 months)

data.symptoms: Progressive cognitive impairment, behavioural changes, motor abnormalities
data.treatment_drugs: ART; supportive care; treat psychotic/depressive features

data.symptoms: Photosensitivity, hyperpigmentation, various rashes
data.treatment_drugs: Sun protection; treat specific infections (see skin section); ART

data.symptoms: Unilateral vesicular rash in dermatome, severe pain (start antivirals within 72hrs)
data.treatment_drugs: Acyclovir or Valacyclovir; Paracetamol or Ibuprofen; Calamine lotion; Amitriptyline (for post-herpetic neuralgia)

data.symptoms: Pain persisting >3 months after rash resolves
data.treatment_drugs: Amitriptyline or Carbamazepine

data.symptoms: Extensive, persistent, erosive mucocutaneous lesions
data.treatment_drugs: Acyclovir or Valacyclovir (episodic or suppressive)

data.symptoms: Greasy, scaly, erythematous patches on scalp (dandruff), eyebrows, nasolabial folds, ears, sternal area, axillae, groin
data.treatment_drugs: Topical hydrocortisone 1% + miconazole cream 2%; Ketoconazole shampoo (scalp)

data.symptoms: Very itchy, symmetrical, discrete scratched pimples/bumps on extremities and trunk (spares face, mucous membranes, palms, webs)
data.treatment_drugs: Medium-to-high potency topical steroids + emollients; Oral antihistamines (chlorpheniramine, promethazine); Calamine lotion

data.symptoms: Purple nodules/lesions (skin, oral palate, internal organs including pulmonary); may be limited or extensive
data.treatment_drugs: ART; chemotherapy (if high tumour burden); biopsy for diagnosis

data.symptoms: KS, cervical cancer, non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL), Hodgkin lymphoma (HL), squamous cell conjunctival carcinoma, lung, breast, prostate, anogenital, colorectal cancers
data.treatment_drugs: ART (continue during cancer treatment, avoid zidovudine); chemotherapy/surgery/radiotherapy (specialist)

data.symptoms: Cough, nasal congestion, fever, malaise (usually viral)
data.treatment_drugs: No antibiotics; symptomatic treatment (rest, hydration, fever reducers)

data.symptoms: Fever, productive cough, pleuritic chest pain, respiratory distress, confusion (CURB-65 criteria), hypotension
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin IV/IM or Ceftriaxone IV ± Erythromycin; switch to oral amoxicillin; admit if CURB-65 ≥1

data.symptoms: Severe pneumonia, often with empyema, high fever
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin IV or Clindamycin IV (at least 7 days then consider oral)

data.symptoms: Severe pneumonia in debilitated patients, not responding to first-line
data.treatment_drugs: Gentamicin + Ceftriaxone (or based on culture)

data.symptoms: Putrid sputum, fever, weight loss, postural drainage
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin + Metronidazole (4-8 weeks) or Amoxicillin-clavulanic acid; repeat CXR at 6 weeks

data.symptoms: Pus in pleural space, fever, chest pain, respiratory distress
data.treatment_drugs: Chest drain; Benzylpenicillin + Metronidazole; Cloxacillin if staphylococcal suspected

data.symptoms: Pneumonia presenting ≥48 hours after admission
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin-clavulanic acid IV or Gentamicin + Benzylpenicillin

data.symptoms: Increased dyspnoea, cough, purulent sputum, fever, new infiltrates
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin or Doxycycline; Salbutamol +/- Ipratropium (nebulised/inhaler); Prednisolone (30mg daily for 5 days); controlled oxygen (SpO2 88-92%)

data.symptoms: Chronic dyspnoea, cough, limited activity, frequent exacerbations
data.treatment_drugs: Salbutamol PRN; add Ipratropium or ICS/LABA (Formoterol/Budesonide or Salmeterol/Fluticasone) if frequent exacerbations; pulmonary rehab; vaccines

data.symptoms: Chronic cough with copious purulent sputum, recurrent infections, haemoptysis
data.treatment_drugs: Antibiotics for exacerbations (broad spectrum based on sputum culture); postural drainage; annual flu vaccine; pneumococcal vaccine; inhaled bronchodilators

data.symptoms: Occasional symptoms (once in 3-4 months)
data.treatment_drugs: Low-dose ICS-formoterol as needed (preferred) OR Salbutamol + additional puff of ICS (e.g., beclomethasone) whenever salbutamol used

data.symptoms: Daytime symptoms >2/week but not daily
data.treatment_drugs: Low-dose ICS (beclomethasone/budesonide) daily + Salbutamol + additional ICS puff whenever salbutamol used

data.symptoms: Daily symptoms, exacerbations >1/year
data.treatment_drugs: Medium-dose ICS (beclomethasone) daily + Salbutamol + additional ICS puff OR ICS/LABA (budesonide-formoterol) as maintenance and reliever

data.symptoms: Persistent symptoms despite moderate therapy
data.treatment_drugs: High-dose ICS/LABA (budesonide-formoterol or salmeterol/fluticasone) as maintenance and reliever; consider low-dose prednisolone; add Theophylline SR (alternative)

data.symptoms: Progressive dyspnoea, wheeze, use of accessory muscles, pulsus paradoxus, PEF <50% predicted
data.treatment_drugs: Nebulised Salbutamol + Ipratropium; Oxygen (6L/min); Prednisolone 40mg daily (5 days); Adrenaline SC (if no nebuliser); severe: Hydrocortisone IV, Magnesium sulphate IV; ICU if tiring/confused/rising pCO2

data.symptoms: Similar to adults; <5yrs: 2.5mg salbutamol, >5yrs: 5mg salbutamol
data.treatment_drugs: Nebulised Salbutamol; Oxygen; Prednisolone (1-2mg/kg for 3-5 days); Adrenaline SC if poor response; severe: Hydrocortisone IV then prednisolone

data.symptoms: Asymptomatic; high BP readings (systolic ≥140 or diastolic ≥90)
data.treatment_drugs: First-line combination: Thiazide (HCT) + CCB (Amlodipine/Nifedipine) OR CCB + ACEi/ARB OR Thiazide + ACEi/ARB; add statin for high CV risk; antiplatelet for secondary prevention

data.symptoms: Hypertensive encephalopathy, LV failure/pulmonary oedema, aortic dissection, severe pre-eclampsia
data.treatment_drugs: Labetalol IV or Nicardipine IV infusion or Dihydralazine IV/IM; frequent BP monitoring

data.disease: Cardiac failure (heart failure with reduced ejection fraction - HFrEF)
data.symptoms: Shortness of breath (exertion/rest), ankle swelling, ascites, fatigue, elevated JVP
data.treatment_drugs: Frusemide + ACEi/ARB (Enalapril/Losartan) or ARNI (Sacubitril/Valsartan) + Beta-blocker (Carvedilol/Bisoprolol/Metoprolol succinate) + MRA (Spironolactone) + SGLT2i (Dapagliflozin); Digoxin if persistent symptoms/atrial fibrillation

data.symptoms: Severe dyspnoea, orthopnoea, pink frothy sputum, crackles, hypoxia
data.treatment_drugs: Prop up; High-flow oxygen; Morphine IV; Metoclopramide (for vomiting); Frusemide IV

data.symptoms: Chest pain/pressure on exertion, relieved by rest/GTN
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin + Atorvastatin/Rosuvastatin + Glyceryl trinitrate SL (PRN)

data.symptoms: More frequent angina episodes
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin + Isosorbide dinitrate + Atenolol +/– Amlodipine/Nifedipine

data.symptoms: Angina at rest or minimal exertion, new onset severe
data.treatment_drugs: Admit; Aspirin + Heparin + Atenolol + Nifedipine/Amlodipine + Isosorbide dinitrate/GTN IV + Atorvastatin/Rosuvastatin

data.symptoms: Chest pain, dyspnoea, nausea, anxiety, ECG changes (ST elevation)
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin (300mg then 75-150mg) + Clopidogrel (300mg then 75mg) + Morphine IV + Atenolol + Enalapril/Lisinopril + Atorvastatin/Rosuvastatin; Thrombolysis (Streptokinase) if <12 hours

data.symptoms: Ectopic beats, atrial fibrillation, atrial flutter, SVT, VT
data.treatment_drugs: Atenolol; Verapamil (SVT); Digoxin (AF/flutter); Amiodarone or Lignocaine (VT); DC cardioversion if unstable

data.symptoms: Irregularly irregular pulse, palpitations, dyspnoea, fatigue, risk of stroke
data.treatment_drugs: Rate control: Atenolol/Bisoprolol/Verapamil ± Digoxin; Anticoagulation: Warfarin (target INR 2-3) or Rivaroxaban or Apixaban (based on CHA2DS2-VASc score)

data.symptoms: Fever, heart murmur, splinter haemorrhages, positive blood cultures (≥3 sets)
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin or Ceftriaxone + Gentamicin (4-6 weeks)

data.symptoms: Fever, heart murmur, signs of systemic infection
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin + Gentamicin (4-6 weeks)

data.symptoms: Fever, migratory polyarthritis, carditis, chorea, erythema marginatum, subcutaneous nodules
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin or Amoxicillin or Erythromycin (single dose) + Aspirin (arthritis); Prednisolone (severe carditis)

data.symptoms: Involuntary jerky movements, muscle weakness, emotional lability
data.treatment_drugs: Haloperidol or Sodium valproate

data.symptoms: History of rheumatic fever or rheumatic heart valve lesions
data.treatment_drugs: Benzathine penicillin monthly (or Amoxicillin/Erythromycin daily) – lifelong if significant murmurs at age 21

data.symptoms: Heartburn, acid regurgitation, odynophagia, hoarse voice, cough
data.treatment_drugs: Lifestyle measures; Magnesium trisilicate + aluminium hydroxide (mild); Omeprazole (moderate/severe); Ranitidine (if no response to omeprazole)

data.symptoms: Chronic upper abdominal pain, postprandial fullness, early satiety, bloating, nausea
data.treatment_drugs: Test and treat H. pylori; PPI trial (6 weeks); Hyoscine; Metoclopramide; refer if alarm features (bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, vomiting, mass)

data.symptoms: Epigastric pain (burning, gnawing), bloating, nausea
data.treatment_drugs: H. pylori eradication: Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin + Omeprazole (2 weeks) OR Amoxicillin + Metronidazole + Omeprazole (if clarithromycin unsuitable)

data.symptoms: Gastric/duodenal ulcer in NSAID user (withdrawal of NSAID)
data.treatment_drugs: Withdraw NSAID; Omeprazole (4 weeks)

data.symptoms: Acute diarrhoea ± vomiting, dehydration
data.treatment_drugs: Rehydration (oral/IV); Prochlorperazine or Metoclopramide (antiemetics, adults only); Codeine or Loperamide (antidiarrhoeal, NOT in children)

data.symptoms: Bloody diarrhoea, fever, abdominal cramps
data.treatment_drugs: Ciprofloxacin or Ceftriaxone; avoid antimotility drugs

data.symptoms: Rice-watery diarrhoea, severe dehydration, vomiting
data.treatment_drugs: Rehydration (IV Ringer's lactate/ORS); Antibiotics: Ciprofloxacin or Azithromycin (after rehydration)

data.symptoms: Bloody diarrhoea, abdominal pain, tenesmus
data.treatment_drugs: Metronidazole

data.symptoms: Right upper quadrant pain, fever, hepatomegaly
data.treatment_drugs: Metronidazole + Ampicillin or Ceftriaxone or Ciprofloxacin; +/- drainage

data.symptoms: Right upper quadrant pain, fever, hepatomegaly
data.treatment_drugs: Metronidazole

data.symptoms: Abdominal pain, diarrhoea and/or constipation, no weight loss, normal labs
data.treatment_drugs: Reassurance; Loperamide (diarrhoea); Laxatives (constipation); Antispasmodics; low dose amitriptyline (pain)

data.symptoms: Infrequent hard stools, straining, sensation of incomplete evacuation
data.treatment_drugs: Lifestyle/dietary measures; Glycerine suppository; Liquid paraffin; Bisacodyl

data.symptoms: Macrocytic anaemia, neurological symptoms (paraesthesia, weakness, ataxia)
data.treatment_drugs: Vitamin B12 injections (hydroxocobalamin) lifelong

data.symptoms: Diarrhoea, abdominal cramps, bloating, foul-smelling stools
data.treatment_drugs: Metronidazole

data.symptoms: Diarrhoea, weight loss, nutritional deficiencies (lactase deficiency, coeliac, pancreatic insufficiency)
data.treatment_drugs: Specialist referral; treat specific cause

data.symptoms: Epigastric pain radiating to back, malabsorption, diabetes, history of alcohol/gallstones
data.treatment_drugs: Pain control (opiates); Pancreatic enzyme supplements; Acid suppression; stop alcohol; refer

data.symptoms: Severe abdominal pain, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, fever
data.treatment_drugs: Ampicillin + Gentamicin + Metronidazole (IV); surgical evaluation

data.symptoms: Perianal pain, bleeding with defecation, itching, discomfort
data.treatment_drugs: High fibre, fluids; Benzyl benzoate with 0.25% hydrocortisone ointment; saline baths

data.symptoms: Confusion, disorientation, asterixis, jaundice, altered consciousness (delirium)
data.treatment_drugs: Identify/eliminate precipitant (bleeding, infection, electrolytes); Lactulose; Magnesium trisilicate; Vitamin K; treat infection; thiamine if alcohol aetiology

data.symptoms: Haematemesis, melaena, signs of chronic liver disease
data.treatment_drugs: IV access, transfusion; Ceftriaxone; Diazepam (sedation); Propranolol prophylaxis; refer for endoscopy

data.symptoms: Abdominal distension, shifting dullness, peripheral oedema
data.treatment_drugs: Salt/fluid restriction; Spironolactone; Frusemide (if spironolactone fails); diagnostic paracentesis

data.symptoms: Ascites with polymorph count >250 cells/µL, abdominal pain, fever, worsening encephalopathy
data.treatment_drugs: IV Ceftriaxone or oral Ciprofloxacin

data.symptoms: Dysuria, frequency, urgency, suprapubic pain, cloudy urine
data.treatment_drugs: Nitrofurantoin (100mg BD for 3 days) or Amoxicillin

data.symptoms: UTI symptoms + nausea, vomiting, fever, rigors, loin pain
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin-clavulanic acid (2 weeks)

data.symptoms: As above, acutely ill, unable to take oral
data.treatment_drugs: Ceftriaxone IV or Gentamicin IV (then switch to oral)

data.symptoms: Fever, specific urinary symptoms, or non-specific symptoms at high risk of serious illness
data.treatment_drugs: Ceftriaxone IV for at least 3 days then oral amoxicillin-clavulanic acid (total 2 weeks)

data.disease: UTI in children (>3 months, upper tract/pyelonephritis)
data.symptoms: Fever ≥38°C + bacteriuria (or loin pain/tenderness)
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin-clavulanic acid or Cefuroxime po (7-10 days); if vomiting: Ceftriaxone IV then oral

data.disease: UTI in children (>3 months, lower tract/cystitis)
data.symptoms: Bacteriuria, no systemic features
data.treatment_drugs: Oral antibiotics for 3 days

data.symptoms: Oliguria, fluid overload, uraemia, history of hypovolaemia/hypotension, sepsis, nephrotoxins
data.treatment_drugs: Fluid challenge (Ringer's lactate or 0.9% NaCl); Frusemide (if euvolemic); treat hyperkalaemia (calcium gluconate, insulin+glucose, salbutamol nebulised, calcium resonium enema); refer/dialyse if deteriorating

data.symptoms: Facial/generalised oedema, oliguria, hypertension, haematuria, proteinuria
data.treatment_drugs: Frusemide; Amoxicillin (if post-streptococcal); treat hypertension; refer if renal function deteriorating

data.symptoms: Rapid decrease in GFR (≥50% over days to 3 months) + features of glomerulonephritis (oedema, haematuria, oliguria, hypertension) – RENAL EMERGENCY
data.treatment_drugs: Methylprednisolone IV (3 days) then Prednisolone (2 weeks) + Calcium + Vitamin D + Omeprazole; URGENT referral

data.symptoms: Generalised oedema, hypoalbuminaemia, proteinuria (>3g/day), hyperlipidaemia
data.treatment_drugs: Frusemide; Prednisolone (2 months, then taper); Enalapril (for proteinuria); Heparin (if immobile); refer if no response in 2 weeks

data.symptoms: Oedema, hypoalbuminaemia (<2.5g/dl), nephrotic range proteinuria (dipstick 3+/4+ or UPCR >2mg/mg)
data.treatment_drugs: Prednisolone (2mg/kg/day for 4-6 weeks, then 1.5mg/kg alternate days for 4-6 weeks) + Calcium + Vitamin D + Omeprazole; Frusemide for severe oedema

data.symptoms: As above in infant
data.treatment_drugs: Refer to nephrologist

data.symptoms: GFR <15ml/min, needing chronic dialysis
data.treatment_drugs: Chronic dialysis (haemodialysis/peritoneal dialysis) after careful selection; no renal transplantation in Zimbabwe

data.symptoms: Hot, swollen, painful joint, fever, reduced range of motion, acute onset
data.treatment_drugs: Surgical drainage; Cloxacillin IV or Clindamycin IV or Vancomycin (4-6 weeks)

data.symptoms: Bone pain, fever, tenderness, swelling, overlying erythema
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin or Clindamycin IV (4-6 weeks); switch to oral when improved

data.symptoms: Persistent bone pain, sinus tract, recurrent infection
data.treatment_drugs: Surgery (antibiotics alone not recommended)

data.symptoms: Fracture with skin breach, risk of infection
data.treatment_drugs: Careful debridement; Cloxacillin or Clindamycin IV (5 days)

data.symptoms: Pain, swelling, deformity, loss of function
data.treatment_drugs: Pain relief (analgesics); splinting/reduction; nil by mouth if surgery planned

data.symptoms: Pain localised to spine ± radiation (exclude fracture, neurology, infection)
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin, Paracetamol, Ibuprofen, or Diclofenac (acute); lowest effective dose (chronic)

data.symptoms: Sudden severe joint pain (often 1st MTP), redness, swelling, warmth
data.treatment_drugs: Diclofenac or Prednisolone or Colchicine (DO NOT start allopurinol during acute attack)

data.symptoms: Tophi, recurrent attacks (>2/year), hyperuricaemia, kidney stones, CKD
data.treatment_drugs: Allopurinol (start 100mg daily, increase by 100mg every 2 weeks to 300mg) + concurrent NSAID/colchicine for first 3 months

data.symptoms: Symmetrical polyarthritis (small joints of hands/feet), morning stiffness >30 min, fatigue, joint erosions
data.treatment_drugs: NSAIDs (Ibuprofen/Diclofenac) + DMARD (Methotrexate once weekly + Folate 2-3 times/week OR Hydroxychloroquine/Chloroquine); Prednisolone low dose (bridge); refer

data.symptoms: Similar to RA but in children
data.treatment_drugs: As for RA; NSAIDs + DMARDs; avoid aspirin (Reye's)

data.symptoms: Malar rash, photosensitivity, arthritis, serositis, renal, neurological, haematological involvement
data.treatment_drugs: Hydroxychloroquine or Chloroquine (skin/joint); Prednisolone (severe/complications); Azathioprine or Mycophenolate mofetil (steroid-sparing); refer

data.symptoms: Joint pain worse with use, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, bony enlargement
data.treatment_drugs: Ibuprofen or Diclofenac PRN; lifestyle measures; physiotherapy

data.symptoms: Urethritis, conjunctivitis, asymmetric arthritis (often after STI or dysentery)
data.treatment_drugs: NSAIDs (as for osteoarthritis); exclude HIV, UTI, bowel infection

data.symptoms: Acute onset, weight loss, ketonuria, polyuria, polydipsia, hyperglycaemia, DKA risk
data.treatment_drugs: Insulin (Basal-bolus regimen: long-acting basal + rapid-acting bolus before meals); Multiple Daily Injections (MDII)

data.symptoms: Insidious onset, often asymptomatic, obesity, hyperglycaemia, sometimes polyuria/polydipsia
data.treatment_drugs: Metformin (first line); add Gliclazide/Glimepiride or Vildagliptin if HbA1c not controlled; add basal insulin (NPH, Detemir, Glargine, Degludec) or premixed insulin if failing oral agents; Dapagliflozin (for those with HF or CKD)

data.symptoms: Diabetes first diagnosed in 2nd/3rd trimester of pregnancy
data.treatment_drugs: Metformin (first line) or Insulin; strict glycaemic control

data.symptoms: Sweating, pallor, tremor, palpitations, tachycardia, hunger (adrenergic); confusion, seizures, coma (neuroglycopaenic); capillary glucose <3.9mmol/L
data.treatment_drugs: Oral fast-acting sugar (if conscious); Glucagon IM (if unconscious/severely impaired); Dextrose 50% IV (if unconscious and IV access); Dextrose 5% infusion

data.symptoms: Hyperglycaemia, ketosis/ketonuria, metabolic acidosis, dehydration, altered consciousness, Kussmaul breathing
data.treatment_drugs: IV fluids (0.9% saline) + Insulin (fixed rate IV infusion 0.1 units/kg/hr) + Potassium replacement; change to 5-10% dextrose when glucose <14mmol/L

data.symptoms: Severe hyperglycaemia, hyperosmolality, severe dehydration, no significant ketosis, altered consciousness
data.treatment_drugs: IV fluids first (0.9% saline), THEN insulin (0.05 units/kg/hr)

data.symptoms: Enlarged thyroid, euthyroid, may have cosmetic or pressure symptoms
data.treatment_drugs: Thyroxine (if symptomatic); refer for workup (TSH, ultrasound)

data.symptoms: Weight loss, heat intolerance, palpitations, tremor, goitre, exophthalmos, anxiety
data.treatment_drugs: Carbimazole; Propranolol (symptom relief until euthyroid); refer for radioiodine or surgery if poor response

data.symptoms: Single or multiple nodules, hyperthyroidism
data.treatment_drugs: Carbimazole (short-term); Radioiodine or surgery (render euthyroid first)

data.symptoms: Weight gain, cold intolerance, fatigue, dry skin, bradycardia, constipation, cognitive slowing
data.treatment_drugs: Thyroxine replacement (start 50-100mcg, adjust to TSH)

data.symptoms: Fatigue, hyperpigmentation (especially scars/creases), hypotension, salt craving, hypoglycaemia, hyponatraemia, hyperkalaemia
data.treatment_drugs: Hydrocortisone (acute crisis: IV 100mg stat + IV fluids); then maintenance (prednisolone/hydrocortisone); Fludrocortisone (if mineralocorticoid deficiency)

data.symptoms: Systolic BP <100mmHg, hypotension, shock, hyponatraemia, hyperkalaemia in known or suspected adrenal insufficiency
data.treatment_drugs: Hydrocortisone IV 100mg stat; IV 0.9% Normal saline (1 litre in first hour)

data.symptoms: Fever, headache, neck stiffness, altered consciousness, seizures, focal signs, vomiting, photophobia
data.treatment_drugs: Benzylpenicillin + Chloramphenicol or Ceftriaxone (14 days); Chemoprophylaxis for contacts (ceftriaxone single dose)

data.symptoms: Subacute meningitis, often with cranial nerve palsies, may present acutely
data.treatment_drugs: Anti-TB treatment (2HRZE/10HR)

data.symptoms: Focal contrast-enhancing lesion(s) on CT, focal neurological signs, headache, confusion, seizures
data.treatment_drugs: Sulphadiazine + Pyrimethamine or Clindamycin + Pyrimethamine or Cotrimoxazole (6 weeks)

data.symptoms: Focal seizures without fever, typical CT scan appearance (cystic lesions)
data.treatment_drugs: Albendazole + Praziquantel + Prednisolone (if drowsiness, seizures, or focal signs develop)

data.symptoms: Bilateral dull, band-like pain, worse as day progresses, no nausea, may be daily
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin PRN (max 1 week continuous); Amitriptyline (if chronic >6 weeks); lifestyle changes, relaxation

data.symptoms: Unilateral throbbing, nausea/vomiting, photophobia, ± aura, lasts hours-days
data.treatment_drugs: Ibuprofen/Aspirin/Paracetamol + Metoclopramide (acute); Ergotamine (if ineffective, contraindicated in complicated migraine); Propranolol or Amitriptyline (prophylaxis if ≥2 disabling migraines/month)

data.symptoms: Unilateral severe periorbital pain, periodicity (weeks), male predominance
data.treatment_drugs: Treat as migraine (no specific prophylaxis mentioned)

data.symptoms: Sudden loss of consciousness, tonic-clonic movements, urinary incontinence, tongue biting, post-ictal confusion
data.treatment_drugs: Sodium valproate (first line, but avoid in pregnancy); Phenobarbitone; Phenytoin; Carbamazepine; Lamotrigine (second line/special groups)

data.symptoms: Continuous seizure activity >30 minutes, or recurrent seizures without regaining consciousness
data.treatment_drugs: Diazepam IV/PR (first line); Phenobarbitone IV; Phenytoin IV; Thiopentone + intubation (if refractory)

data.symptoms: Seizures associated with fever (age 6m-6y), no intracranial infection
data.treatment_drugs: Treat fever (paracetamol); Diazepam PR if prolonged; NO routine antiepileptic prophylaxis

data.symptoms: Acute onset disorientation, short-term memory loss, fluctuating consciousness, hallucinations (medical emergency)
data.treatment_drugs: Treat underlying cause (DIMTOP: Drugs, Infection, Metabolic, Trauma, Oxygen deficit, Psychiatric/Pain); Haloperidol IM or Diazepam/Midazolam IV/IM (if very restless); Chlorpromazine IM (if sedation required)

data.symptoms: Progressive cognitive decline, memory loss, impairment in daily activities, behavioural changes (reversible causes: hypothyroidism, B12 deficiency, pellagra, neurosyphilis, HIV, normal pressure hydrocephalus, depression)
data.treatment_drugs: Treat reversible causes; donepezil (for Alzheimer's, specialist initiation); psychosocial support; manage behavioural symptoms with low-dose antipsychotics (e.g., sulpiride, quetiapine, haloperidol) or low-dose benzodiazepines PRN

data.symptoms: Sudden onset focal neurological deficit (hemiparesis, aphasia, facial droop, gaze abnormality, arm drift), NIHSS or G-FAST score
data.treatment_drugs: Aspirin (150mg pre-referral if not contraindicated); thrombolysis not practical; manage BP; nil by mouth until swallow assessed; CT brain to exclude haemorrhage

data.symptoms: After TIA or minor ischaemic stroke
data.treatment_drugs: Clopidogrel + Aspirin (dual antiplatelet for 21 days) then Clopidogrel alone; high-intensity statin (Atorvastatin); BP lowering (thiazide/CCB/ACEi); anticoagulation if atrial fibrillation

data.symptoms: Ascending weakness, paraesthesia, areflexia, may progress to respiratory failure
data.treatment_drugs: Supportive care; IV immunoglobulins (severe cases); NO steroids

data.symptoms: Fatigable weakness, ptosis, diplopia, difficulty swallowing/speaking
data.treatment_drugs: Refer for diagnosis (specialist)

data.symptoms: Burning, numbness, paraesthesia in glove-and-stocking distribution (causes: diabetes, HIV, alcohol, B12 deficiency, drugs)
data.treatment_drugs: Amitriptyline (first line); add Carbamazepine if ineffective; Codeine or Morphine for severe pain

data.symptoms: Fine bilateral postural tremor (present on maintaining posture, stops at rest)
data.treatment_drugs: Propranolol

data.symptoms: Coarse resting tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, postural instability (exclude drug-induced)
data.treatment_drugs: Benzhexol (initial tremor); Levodopa/Carbidopa (when motor symptoms interfere with daily activities); refer

data.symptoms: Intention tremor, gait ataxia, nystagmus
data.treatment_drugs: Refer for CT/MRI

data.symptoms: Hallucinations (auditory), delusions (persecutory/grandiose), disorganised speech/behaviour, negative symptoms (apathy, social withdrawal), loss of contact with reality
data.treatment_drugs: Chlorpromazine, Haloperidol, Trifluoperazine, Sulpiride (first line); Olanzapine, Risperidone, Quetiapine, Clozapine (second line, clozapine requires FBC monitoring)

data.symptoms: Agitation, violence, risk to self/others, aggression
data.treatment_drugs: Chlorpromazine IM or Haloperidol IM + Diazepam IM/IV or Lorazepam IM

data.symptoms: Psychosis secondary to known medical condition, antipsychotic side effects more common
data.treatment_drugs: Treat cause; lower doses of atypical antipsychotics (e.g., Risperidone)

data.symptoms: Elevated/expansive mood, grandiosity, decreased need for sleep, pressured speech, racing thoughts, increased goal-directed activity, psychosis
data.treatment_drugs: Mood stabiliser: Carbamazepine, Sodium valproate, Lamotrigine, or Lithium carbonate; Antipsychotic: Olanzapine, Risperidone, Quetiapine (for acute mania)

data.symptoms: Depressed mood, anhedonia, low energy, often with psychomotor retardation
data.treatment_drugs: Antidepressant (e.g., Fluoxetine) + mood stabiliser (avoid carbamazepine in HIV)

data.symptoms: Persistent sad/depressed mood, anhedonia, fatigue, sleep/appetite changes, feelings of worthlessness/guilt, suicidal ideation, multiple unexplained physical symptoms
data.treatment_drugs: Fluoxetine, Citalopram, Sertraline (first line SSRIs); Amitriptyline, Imipramine (tricyclics, avoid in heart disease/glaucoma/epilepsy); Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, Mianserin (second line)

data.symptoms: Depression with restlessness, difficulty sitting still
data.treatment_drugs: Amitriptyline or Imipramine at night

data.symptoms: Depression with slowed thoughts/movements, lethargy
data.treatment_drugs: Fluoxetine in the morning

data.symptoms: Pervasive worry, feeling on edge, restlessness, irritability, multiple physical symptoms (dizziness, shortness of breath, palpitations, abdominal pain)
data.treatment_drugs: Psychosocial interventions; Diazepam or Clonazepam (severe, max 2 weeks); Propranolol (for performance anxiety)

data.symptoms: Compulsive alcohol use, tolerance, withdrawal symptoms (tremors, insomnia, confusion, hallucinations, seizures), deterioration in social functioning
data.treatment_drugs: Diazepam (fixed dose reducing regimen for detoxification) or Chlordiazepoxide; Thiamine + Vitamin B Complex (to prevent Wernicke's encephalopathy); Multivitamins

data.symptoms: Severe confusion, disorientation, hallucinations, agitation, autonomic instability, seizures
data.treatment_drugs: Diazepam IV; ICU setting; high-dose benzodiazepines

data.symptoms: Hallucinations (usually auditory) in clear sensorium, occurring during or after heavy alcohol use
data.treatment_drugs: Low-dose antipsychotic (Chlorpromazine or Risperidone) for severe agitation

data.symptoms: Ophthalmoplegia, ataxia, confusion, altered consciousness (thiamine deficiency)
data.treatment_drugs: Parenteral thiamine (500mg for 3-5 days)

data.disease: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) - children >5yrs & adolescents
data.symptoms: Inattention, hyperactivity, impulsivity, symptoms in multiple settings for >6 months, difficulty with daily functioning
data.treatment_drugs: Methylphenidate (immediate or slow release) or Dextroamphetamine or Atomoxetine (specialist initiation & monitoring)

data.symptoms: Deficits in social communication, repetitive behaviours, resistance to change, irritability, self-injurious behaviour
data.treatment_drugs: Risperidone or Aripiprazole (for severe irritability/aggression, specialist use)

data.symptoms: Repetitive persistent antisocial, aggressive, defiant behaviour violating age-appropriate social rules
data.treatment_drugs: Risperidone or Aripiprazole or Carbamazepine (for aggression not responding to psychosocial interventions)

data.symptoms: Repeated voiding of urine into bed, at least twice/week for ≥3 months, child ≥5 years
data.treatment_drugs: Desmopressin at night (first line); Imipramine (second line for children 6-18 years) – after exclusion of medical causes

data.symptoms: Difficulty sleeping, part of depressive/anxiety symptoms
data.treatment_drugs: Limited use of benzodiazepines (clonazepam or lorazepam) for max 2 weeks

data.symptoms: Severe eye pain, headache, nausea, vomiting, blurred vision with halos around lights, red eye, fixed/dilated pupil, decreased vision
data.treatment_drugs: Pilocarpine 2/4% drops; Timolol 0.5% drops; Acetazolamide po stat then 8 hourly; urgent referral

data.symptoms: Night blindness, conjunctival xerosis (dryness), Bitot's spots, corneal xerosis/ulceration, keratomalacia
data.treatment_drugs: Vitamin A (single dose on Day 1, Day 2, repeat after 2 weeks)

data.symptoms: Red eye, purulent discharge, gritty sensation, eyelids may stick together
data.treatment_drugs: Tetracycline 1% or Chloramphenicol 1% eye ointment (3-4 times daily for one week); eyelid hygiene

data.symptoms: Watery discharge, red eye, self-limiting, often bilateral
data.treatment_drugs: No antibiotics; treat as bacterial if unsure; refer if persists >1 week

data.symptoms: Itching (marked), redness, watering, both eyes, often recurrent
data.treatment_drugs: Olopatadine 1% eye drops; cold compresses; avoid steroids

data.symptoms: Chronic follicular conjunctivitis, corneal scarring, trichiasis (eyelashes touching cornea), entropion
data.treatment_drugs: Tetracycline 1% eye ointment (4 times daily for 6 weeks); epilation for trichiasis; refer

data.symptoms: Conjunctivitis with discharge in neonate within first month of life (gonococcal/chlamydial/bacterial)
data.treatment_drugs: Kanamycin IM + Erythromycin po (treat parents as well); eye irrigation; topical ofloxacin/gentamicin + tetracycline ointment

data.symptoms: History of trauma, visible wound, decreased vision, hyphema, possible prolapse of intraocular contents
data.treatment_drugs: Eye shield; Tetanus vaccine; Paracetamol; Amoxicillin; URGENT referral

data.symptoms: Foreign body sensation, pain, tearing, photophobia
data.treatment_drugs: Attempt removal with topical anaesthetic (lignocaine 1-2%); if successful: tetracycline/chloramphenicol ointment + pad for 24hrs; if unsuccessful refer

data.symptoms: Pain, photophobia, tearing, history of scratch, staining with fluorescein
data.treatment_drugs: Eye pad + tetracycline/chloramphenicol ointment for 24 hours; review; if improving continue ointment for 4-5 days

data.symptoms: Pain, redness, lid swelling, decreased vision, history of chemical exposure
data.treatment_drugs: Immediate irrigation (tap water for ≥15-30 minutes); tetracycline/chloramphenicol ointment; pad eye; urgent referral

data.symptoms: Red eye (circumcorneal injection), deep pain worse with eye movement, photophobia, blurred vision, small/irregular pupil
data.treatment_drugs: Refer to eye specialist

data.symptoms: Red eye, pain, photophobia, corneal opacity with fluorescein staining, decreased vision (refer, test corneal sensation to rule out herpetic cause)
data.treatment_drugs: Tetracycline or Chloramphenicol eye ointment (5-7 days); if herpetic: acyclovir ointment/po

data.symptoms: Asymptomatic initially, then progressive visual field loss, optic disc cupping, ± elevated IOP
data.treatment_drugs: Latanoprost eye drops (first line) or Timolol maleate or Travoprost or Xalacom; add Acetazolamide if IOP >40mmHg (not >3 months without review)

data.symptoms: Progressive painless vision loss, white pupil (in children), squint (children)
data.treatment_drugs: Surgical removal; pre-op: Mydriatics (tropicamide, cyclopentolate, phenylephrine); post-op: corticosteroids (dexamethasone/betamethasone/prednisolone acetate) + antibiotics (gentamicin/ciprofloxacin/ofloxacin)

data.symptoms: Whitish patches (non-adherent, wipe off leaving erythematous surface), reddening, altered taste, angular cheilitis
data.treatment_drugs: Nystatin suspension/lozenges or Miconazole 2% oral gel (7-14 days)

data.symptoms: Odynophagia (pain on swallowing), chest pain, difficulty swallowing (AIDS-defining illness)
data.treatment_drugs: Fluconazole (200mg BD for 7-14 days); refer for ART; cotrimoxazole prophylaxis

data.symptoms: Vesicles on lips, buccal mucosa, hard palate, causing pain/discomfort
data.treatment_drugs: Acyclovir (400mg TDS for 5 days)

data.symptoms: Purple nodules/lesions on palate or under tongue
data.treatment_drugs: Offer HIV testing; cotrimoxazole prophylaxis; refer to OI clinic for ART; biopsy

data.symptoms: Spontaneous bleeding of gums, loosening of teeth, pain, fetor oris
data.treatment_drugs: Metronidazole + Amoxicillin (5 days); antibacterial mouthwash (betadine, chlorhexidine); refer to oral health professional

data.symptoms: Cavities, intrinsic discolouration, toothache
data.treatment_drugs: Oral hygiene (fluoride toothpaste), limit sweets, regular dental exam, refer for restoration/extraction

data.disease: Fascial space abscess / Ludwig's angina / Cervicofacial necrotising fasciitis
data.symptoms: Dental infection, jaw fracture, immunosuppression; swelling, airway compromise (raised tongue, drooling), pain, fever, sepsis
data.treatment_drugs: Surgical source control (drainage, extraction, debridement); Ceftriaxone IV or Clindamycin IV + Metronidazole IV ± Gentamicin; Dexamethasone if airway compromise; refer

data.symptoms: Painful ulcers on buccal mucosa, interfere with eating
data.treatment_drugs: Symptomatic: Chlorhexidine mouth rinse or Povidone iodine or Triamcinolone in orabase; biopsy if large/not healing >14 days

data.symptoms: Nodule or penetrating lesion (hole) on palate
data.treatment_drugs: Ketoconazole (200mg BD for months); biopsy for confirmation

data.symptoms: Ear pain (otalgia), fever, chills, irritability, bulging/inflamed tympanic membrane
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin (7 days) if: age <6 months, severe AOM (axillary temp >39.5°C), comorbidity (malnutrition, HIV), failure to resolve in 48-72 hours, or patient unlikely to return

data.symptoms: More than 4 episodes per year
data.treatment_drugs: REFER

data.symptoms: Fever, chills, tenderness over mastoid, retroauricular swelling, anterior displacement of auricle, bulging/unhealthy TM
data.treatment_drugs: IV antibiotics (Benzylpenicillin or Ceftriaxone) stat; REFER

data.symptoms: Hearing loss, aural fullness, brownish fluid behind intact TM (no acute inflammation)
data.treatment_drugs: REFER (adult: exclude nasopharyngeal carcinoma)

data.symptoms: Otorrhoea >14 days, hearing loss, mucopurulent discharge, ossicular chain erosion
data.treatment_drugs: Aural toilet (saline/acidifying agent); Ciprofloxacin ear drops; Amoxicillin; keep ears dry; REFER to ENT surgeon

data.symptoms: Keratin debris in middle ear, hearing loss, discharge
data.treatment_drugs: REFER immediately

data.symptoms: Itchiness, inflamed ear canal, discharge, pain
data.treatment_drugs: Aural toilet; Ciprofloxacin+dexamethasone or Chloramphenicol+dexamethasone ear drops; Amoxicillin or Ciprofloxacin po (if severe)

data.symptoms: Necrotising infection in immunosuppressed (diabetes, HIV), severe pain, granulation tissue, may have cranial nerve palsies
data.treatment_drugs: IV antibiotics (Benzylpenicillin); IV fluids; debridement; REFER immediately

data.symptoms: Itching, discharge, fungal hyphae
data.treatment_drugs: Clotrimazole ear drops; acidifying agents

data.symptoms: Nasal blockage, rhinorrhoea, anosmia, facial pressure/pain, discharge turns mucopurulent if bacterial
data.treatment_drugs: Supportive care; Amoxicillin (7 days) if: failure to resolve in 48-72 hours, purulent discharge, or comorbidities

data.symptoms: Preseptal/orbital cellulitis, proptosis, visual disturbance, confusion, signs of meningitis, eyelid swelling
data.treatment_drugs: REFER immediately

data.symptoms: Symptoms persisting for 90 days
data.treatment_drugs: REFER

data.symptoms: Clear rhinorrhoea, nasal obstruction, anosmia, sneezing, itching
data.treatment_drugs: Fluticasone nasal spray (1 month); Oral antihistamines (Chlorpheniramine or Cetirizine) if needed

data.symptoms: Nasal blockage, necrosis of mucosa, orbital signs (proptosis, ophthalmoplegia), intracranial extension in immunosuppressed
data.treatment_drugs: URGENT referral; surgical debridement; systemic antifungals

data.symptoms: Fever, chills, odynophagia, dysphagia, inflamed tonsils with exudate
data.treatment_drugs: Amoxicillin (7 days); Amoxicillin/clavulanic acid (second line); peritonsillar abscess: antibiotics + refer

data.symptoms: Bleeding from nose (anterior or posterior), may be severe
data.treatment_drugs: Anterior packing (ribbon gauze); posterior packing (Foley catheter) if posterior bleed; refer if posterior bleed, hypertension, anticoagulation, failed anterior packing, or malignancy suspicion

data.symptoms: Clear rhinorrhoea after head injury/nasal surgery or spontaneous, worsens on leaning forward, headache
data.treatment_drugs: REFER

data.symptoms: Barking cough, inspiratory stridor, fever (grades 1-4)
data.treatment_drugs: Racemic adrenaline nebuliser (grades 1&2); Dexamethasone IV (grades 1&2); Grades 3&4: secure airway, treat and refer

data.symptoms: Rapidly spreading blisters/pustules, honey-coloured crusts, often on face (common in children)
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin po (if severe or systemic symptoms) + hygiene measures (soap and water)

data.symptoms: Small pustules around hair follicles, superficial or deep
data.treatment_drugs: Bathing; treat as impetigo

data.symptoms: Painful deep-seated hair follicle infection (Staph. aureus), often neck/axillae, may have fever/lymphadenopathy
data.treatment_drugs: Hot compresses; Cloxacillin po (if fever/LN swelling); surgical incision if not improving

data.symptoms: Raised, sharply demarcated, red, hot, swollen area (often face/leg), high fever, pain
data.treatment_drugs: Erythromycin or Benzathine penicillin (7 days; 10-14 days if recurrent)

data.symptoms: Diffuse, indistinct redness, swelling, warmth, pain (deeper than erysipelas)
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin po or Azithromycin (5-7 days)

data.symptoms: Painful red swelling of nailfold, may have pus
data.treatment_drugs: Erythromycin or Cloxacillin (5-7 days); incision & drainage if abscess

data.symptoms: Chronic nailfold swelling, often candida
data.treatment_drugs: Avoid water/trauma; treat secondary bacterial infection; topical antifungals

data.symptoms: Comedones, papulopustules, nodules on face/chest/back
data.treatment_drugs: Benzoyl peroxide 5% gel (mild); Doxycycline (100mg daily for 2-4 months for severe nodular acne)

data.symptoms: Round, expanding lesions with white, dust-like scales, distinct borders
data.treatment_drugs: Miconazole 2% or Clotrimazole 1% cream (apply until 7 days after resolution)

data.symptoms: Itchy, scaly, cracked, macerated skin between toes
data.treatment_drugs: Miconazole or Clotrimazole cream (apply until 7 days after resolution); keep feet dry; Griseofulvin (severe)

data.symptoms: Hypopigmented patches on chest/back/arms (yeast infection)
data.treatment_drugs: Selenium sulphide 2% shampoo or Miconazole shampoo (daily for 5 days)

data.symptoms: Scaly, itchy scalp with patchy hair loss, broken hairs (fungus in hair follicle)
data.treatment_drugs: Griseofulvin (500mg daily for 14 days); topical antifungals NOT effective

data.symptoms: Intense itching (worse at night), burrows (whitish zig-zag) in interdigital spaces, wrists, axillae, navel, genitals
data.treatment_drugs: Permethrin 5% cream (single application, wash off after 8-12hrs) or Gamma benzene hexachloride 1% lotion or Benzyl benzoate 25% emulsion; treat all close contacts; wash bedding/clothes

data.symptoms: Vesicles on lips/mouth (or genitals), may recur with stress/illness
data.treatment_drugs: Keep lesions dry; no specific medication (acyclovir for severe HIV-related)

data.symptoms: Fever, widespread vesicles (trunk → face/scalp) at different stages (papules, vesicles, crusts)
data.treatment_drugs: Calamine lotion; Chlorpheniramine (for itching)

data.symptoms: Unilateral dermatomal vesicular rash, severe pain (see HIV section)
data.treatment_drugs: Acyclovir (within 72hrs) or Valacyclovir; Paracetamol/Ibuprofen; Calamine lotion; Amitriptyline (post-herpetic neuralgia)

data.symptoms: Red, itchy, weeping, scaling, crusted lesions, often flexural (elbows, knees, neck) in older children/adults
data.treatment_drugs: Emulsifying ointment or Aqueous cream (soap substitute); Topical hydrocortisone 1% (dry eczema - ointment, weepy eczema - cream); Antihistamines (chlorpheniramine/promethazine for itching); treat secondary infection

data.symptoms: Itchy, raised wheals (hives) due to dermal oedema (allergic or physical causes)
data.treatment_drugs: Chlorpheniramine or Promethazine or Cetirizine po; avoid topical antihistamines; identify/eliminate trigger

data.symptoms: Thick, silvery-scaly, erythematous plaques, symmetrical (elbows, knees, scalp, sacrum)
data.treatment_drugs: Salicylic acid 2% ointment (keratolytic); Coal tar 5% + salicylic acid 2% ointment; Zinc oxide ointment; sun exposure; avoid steroids

data.symptoms: Diarrhoea, dermatitis (sun-exposed areas, pressure points), dementia
data.treatment_drugs: Nicotinamide (100mg daily for 2 weeks or until review); high-protein diet

data.symptoms: Generalised (albinism) or patchy (vitiligo) loss of pigmentation
data.treatment_drugs: Sunscreen (PABA cream/lotion) and sun blocker (balm on lips) daily; protective clothing; yearly skin cancer screening

data.symptoms: Keratotic papules (common) or endophytic lesions on soles (plantar)
data.treatment_drugs: Leave to resolve spontaneously; refer if extensive

data.symptoms: Skin injury from heat (fire, hot liquids, hot objects), pain (superficial), blistering, eschar (deep), erythema
data.treatment_drugs: Immediate cooling (water 25°C for 15-30 min); Paracetamol + Codeine (mild/moderate pain); Morphine IV (severe pain, large burns); Tetanus toxoid; Silver sulfadiazine 1% or Povidone-iodine 5% cream (topical); IV Ringer's lactate (large burns, Parkland formula); antibiotics if contaminated (benzylpenicillin or erythromycin then based on C&S)

data.symptoms: Tissue damage from acids or alkalis (battery acid, drain cleaner, bleach, etc.)
data.treatment_drugs: Brush off dry powder; copious water irrigation (≥20 minutes); soft paraffin on insoluble residues; calcium gluconate gel for hydrofluoric acid

data.symptoms: Entry/exit wounds, possible cardiac arrhythmia, unconsciousness, muscle damage
data.treatment_drugs: Cool burns; cardiac assessment and resuscitation (defibrillation/CPR if needed)

data.symptoms: Respiratory distress, stridor, hoarseness, cough, shortness of breath, soot/burns around mouth/nose, history of enclosed space
data.treatment_drugs: 100% oxygen; airway management; monitor for respiratory failure

data.symptoms: Pain score 1-3
data.treatment_drugs: Paracetamol or Ibuprofen or Diclofenac (oral)

data.symptoms: Pain score 4-6
data.treatment_drugs: Add Codeine or Tramadol to mild pain regimen

data.symptoms: Pain score ≥7
data.treatment_drugs: Morphine (oral short-acting 5-10mg 4 hourly, OR long-acting 12 hourly); plus breakthrough doses (60-100% of 4-hourly dose); plus laxative for constipation, antiemetic for nausea (metoclopramide/prochlorperazine/haloperidol)

data.symptoms: Burning, shooting, stabbing pain (trigeminal neuralgia, post-herpetic neuralgia, peripheral neuropathy)
data.treatment_drugs: Amitriptyline or Carbamazepine or Gabapentin

data.disease: Nerve compression pain (e.g., spinal cord compression)
data.symptoms: Pain due to tumour or oedema compressing nerve/spinal cord, may have neurological deficit
data.treatment_drugs: Dexamethasone IV stat (16mg) then prednisolone; refer urgently

data.symptoms: Headache, nausea, vomiting, altered consciousness due to tumour oedema
data.treatment_drugs: Dexamethasone or Prednisolone

data.symptoms: Pain score 1-3 (age-appropriate assessment)
data.treatment_drugs: Paracetamol (10-15mg/kg 4 hourly)

data.symptoms: Pain score ≥7
data.treatment_drugs: Paracetamol + Morphine (0.02-0.04mg/kg 4 hourly)

data.symptoms: Agitated delirium, cognitive impairment, respiratory secretions, urinary problems, pain, dyspnoea in last hours of life
data.treatment_drugs: Focus on comfort: Atropine or Hyoscine (for secretions); Morphine (pain/dyspnoea); Benzodiazepines (anxiety/agitation); discontinue IV fluids, unnecessary medications; involve family

data.symptoms: Falls, immobility, delirium, incontinence, susceptibility to medication side effects, weight loss, functional decline
data.treatment_drugs: Comprehensive Geriatric Assessment (CGA); deprescribing; exercise; nutrition; treat underlying causes

data.symptoms: Fracture from standing height or less (especially vertebral, hip, humerus), low bone mass, height loss, kyphosis
data.treatment_drugs: Alendronate (70mg once weekly) or Zoledronic acid IV (5mg every 12-18 months); Calcium + Vitamin D; lifestyle (weight-bearing exercise, diet, sun exposure)

data.symptoms: Acute onset fluctuating confusion, inattention, disorientation, altered consciousness (often due to infection, medication, dehydration, pain, constipation)
data.treatment_drugs: Treat underlying cause; Haloperidol (low dose) or Lorazepam (PRN for severe agitation/risk of harm); non-pharmacological management (reorientation, calm environment)

data.symptoms: Progressive memory loss, cognitive decline, impairment in daily activities, behavioural changes (apathy, irritability, loss of emotional control)
data.treatment_drugs: Donepezil (specialist initiation, trial for 2-3 months, discontinue if no benefit)

data.symptoms: Urgency, frequency, nocturia, urge incontinence
data.treatment_drugs: Pelvic floor exercises, bladder training; Mirabegron (50mg daily, trial for 2-3 months); avoid oxybutynin (high anticholinergic burden)

data.symptoms: Hard stools, infrequent bowel movements, difficulty passing stool, risk of faecal impaction → overflow diarrhoea
data.treatment_drugs: Hydration, mobilisation, high-fibre diet; Senna or Bisacodyl (stimulant laxatives); Glycerine suppositories; review medications (opiates, CCBs, iron)

data.symptoms: Early: nausea, vomiting; later (3-4 days): liver damage (jaundice, coagulopathy, hepatic failure)
data.treatment_drugs: Activated charcoal (single dose within 2 hours); Acetylcysteine IV (if >10g ingested or high plasma level, effective up to 36 hours)

data.symptoms: Tinnitus, hyperventilation (respiratory alkalosis then metabolic acidosis), fever, confusion, vomiting, dehydration
data.treatment_drugs: Activated charcoal (within 4 hours); Dextrose 5% + Sodium bicarbonate infusion (if severe); Frusemide (if fluid retention)

data.symptoms: Headache, weakness, dizziness, tachycardia, tachypnoea, confusion, coma, cherry-red skin (late)
data.treatment_drugs: 100% oxygen; remove from exposure; Frusemide + Hydrocortisone (cerebral oedema); Diazepam (convulsions)

data.symptoms: Difficulty breathing, drowsiness, blurring of vision, hypotension, cardiac arrhythmias, convulsions (can be fatal with as little as 2g)
data.treatment_drugs: Orogastric tube + gastric lavage; Activated charcoal; Diazepam IV (convulsions and cardioprotective); supportive care

data.symptoms: CNS depression, coma, hypoglycaemia, metabolic acidosis, hypothermia
data.treatment_drugs: Gastric lavage (if soon after ingestion); Naloxone (if opiate co-ingestion); Dextrose 50% then Dextrose 5% infusion; Thiamine; Sodium bicarbonate (if acidosis)

data.symptoms: SLUDGE syndrome: salivation, lacrimation, urination, defecation, GI upset, emesis; miosis, muscle fasciculations, weakness, confusion, respiratory depression
data.treatment_drugs: Atropine IV (high doses, repeated until atropinisation: dry skin, dilated pupils, fast pulse); Pralidoxime (for organophosphates, NOT carbamates)

data.symptoms: Multiple organ toxicity, pulmonary fibrosis (delayed, up to 3 weeks), caustic burns to mouth/GI tract
data.treatment_drugs: Gastric lavage; Fuller's earth or Activated charcoal; Hydrocortisone (if severe, early); AVOID oxygen (makes more toxic)

data.symptoms: Aspiration pneumonitis (cough, fever, hypoxia), CNS depression, cardiac arrhythmias
data.treatment_drugs: DO NOT induce vomiting; DO NOT perform gastric lavage; supportive care (monitor for pneumonitis)

data.symptoms: CNS stimulation (seizures, agitation), cardiac arrhythmias (wide QRS, tachycardia), coma, respiratory depression
data.treatment_drugs: Gastric lavage + Activated charcoal; Diazepam IV (seizures); Lignocaine IV (arrhythmias); Sodium bicarbonate IV (for acidosis); avoid vasoconstrictors

data.symptoms: Bleeding (20WBCT positive), neurotoxicity (ptosis, dysphagia, respiratory distress), severe swelling of bitten limb, tissue necrosis
data.treatment_drugs: Pressure bandage/immobilisation (first aid); Polyvalent snake antivenom IV (40ml, repeat as needed); Tetanus toxoid; no test dose; monitor for anaphylaxis

data.symptoms: Severe local pain, neurotoxic effects (agitation, hypersalivation, respiratory distress), cardiotoxic effects
data.treatment_drugs: Scorpion anti-venom (check manufacturer); Atropine (if cholinergic signs); symptomatic management; refer if severe

data.symptoms: GI symptoms (nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea) after 6-12 hours, then delayed hepatotoxicity, renal failure, hypoglycaemia, acidosis (24-72 hours)
data.treatment_drugs: Gastric lavage + Activated charcoal (within 4 hours); supportive care; no effective antidote; monitor liver/kidney function

data.symptoms: Sudden severe abdominal pain (<7-10 days), colicky or sharp, vomiting, constipation/obstipation, distension, failure to pass flatus
data.treatment_drugs: Resuscitation (IV fluids, NGT, urinary catheter); broad-spectrum antibiotics (if peritonitis/suspected perforation); treat specific cause after diagnostic workup

data.symptoms: Periumbilical pain shifting to right iliac fossa (McBurney's point tenderness), nausea, vomiting, anorexia, low-grade fever, ± Rovsing's sign
data.treatment_drugs: Emergency appendicectomy; Prophylaxis: Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole (single dose pre-op)

data.symptoms: Palpable mass in right iliac fossa, clinical signs of appendicitis but localized
data.treatment_drugs: IV antibiotics (Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin + Metronidazole or Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole); serial examinations; elective appendicectomy in 6 weeks if improves

data.symptoms: Abscess in right iliac fossa, fever, localised peritonitis
data.treatment_drugs: Percutaneous or surgical drainage + IV antibiotics (as for mass); interval appendicectomy may not be necessary

data.symptoms: Generalised peritonitis (board-like rigidity, rebound tenderness, absent bowel sounds), septic shock
data.treatment_drugs: Aggressive fluid resuscitation; IV antibiotics (as for abscess); urgent laparotomy

data.symptoms: Colicky abdominal pain, vomiting, abdominal distension, absolute constipation/obstipation (no stool/flatus), previous abdominal surgery (adhesions) or hernia
data.treatment_drugs: NGT decompression; IV fluids (replace NGT losses ml/ml); Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin + Metronidazole (if suspected bacterial translocation/gangrene); surgical referral

data.symptoms: Acute right upper quadrant pain (often after fatty meal, at night), fever, Murphy's sign, nausea, vomiting
data.treatment_drugs: Ampicillin + Metronidazole or Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole (IV); analgesia; elective cholecystectomy after 6 weeks (or early "hot" cholecystectomy by specialist)

data.symptoms: Sudden severe epigastric pain spreading to whole abdomen, board-like rigidity, rebound tenderness, free air under diaphragm on erect CXR
data.treatment_drugs: Emergency surgery; resuscitation (IV fluids, NGT, analgesia, catheter); Benzylpenicillin + Gentamicin + Metronidazole (IV)

data.symptoms: Painful, fluctuant swelling in lactating/pregnant woman, overlying erythema, tenderness
data.treatment_drugs: Incision & drainage (under GA); Cloxacillin IV then po or Clindamycin IV then po; continue breastfeeding/expressing

data.symptoms: Painful, swollen, red breast, fever, but no fluctuance/pointing
data.treatment_drugs: Cloxacillin or Clindamycin po (mild); IV if severe; review for development of abscess

data.symptoms: Itchy, scaly, erythematous rash on breast/nipple (usually bilateral; rule out Paget's - usually unilateral)
data.treatment_drugs: Topical corticosteroids (as per skin chapter); biopsy if doubt

data.symptoms: Thyrotoxicosis not responding to medical therapy or radioiodine, confirmed/suspected malignancy, goitre with compressive complications (airway, vessels, nerves)
data.treatment_drugs: Render euthyroid with Carbimazole pre-operatively; emergency cases may need IV Propranolol

data.symptoms: Non-healing wound, exclude malignancy (biopsy), peripheral vascular disease, diabetes mellitus, venous stasis
data.treatment_drugs: Clean with normal saline; glycerine & ichthammol dressing; treat underlying cause; avoid frequent dressing changes

data.symptoms: Fatigue, pallor, weakness, hypochromic microcytic blood picture (MCV <75fL), koilonychia, pica
data.treatment_drugs: Ferrous sulphate (200mg TDS) for 3 months after Hb normalises; treat underlying cause

data.symptoms: Macrocytic anaemia (MCV >105fL), glossitis, paraesthesia, subacute combined degeneration of spinal cord (B12 deficiency)
data.treatment_drugs: Hydroxocobalamin (Vitamin B12) IM (loading then maintenance) + Folic acid (5mg daily for 3 months) – give both to avoid precipitating neuropathy

data.symptoms: Painful crises (vaso-occlusive), chronic haemolytic anaemia, susceptibility to infection (encapsulated organisms), leg ulcers, priapism, acute chest syndrome
data.treatment_drugs: Folic acid (5mg daily lifelong); Penicillin V (250mg daily lifelong); Pyrimethamine/dapsone (malaria prophylaxis in endemic areas); Morphine (severe pain); Amoxicillin (for infections); Hydroxyurea (500mg daily for frequent crises, titrate to HbF)

data.symptoms: Acute haemolytic anaemia (haemoglobinuria, jaundice, fatigue) triggered by oxidant drugs (primaquine, dapsone, sulphonamides, quinolones, nitrofurantoin) or infections (e.g., malaria)
data.treatment_drugs: Stop triggering agent; IV fluids; oral iron + folate; avoid blood transfusion unless severe; Medic-alert bracelet

data.symptoms: Spontaneous or traumatic bleeding into joints (haemarthrosis), muscles, mucous membranes, CNS; prolonged bleeding
data.treatment_drugs: Factor VIII concentrate or Cryoprecipitate (dose based on bleed severity, 14-60 IU/kg); Tranexamic acid (mucosal bleeding/adjunct); ice compression; avoid IM injections/aspirin

data.symptoms: Similar bleeding tendency as Haemophilia A
data.treatment_drugs: Factor IX concentrate or Fresh frozen plasma (4-12 bags depending on bleed severity)

data.symptoms: Mucocutaneous bleeding (epistaxis, menorrhagia, bruising), prolonged bleeding after surgery/trauma
data.treatment_drugs: Factor VIII concentrate (contains vWF) or Cryoprecipitate; Tranexamic acid

data.symptoms: Bleeding (petechiae, ecchymoses, oozing from sites) and/or thrombosis, prolonged PT/APTT, low platelets, low fibrinogen
data.treatment_drugs: Treat underlying cause; Fresh frozen plasma (if bleeding + prolonged PT/APTT); Platelet concentrate (if platelets <30x10⁹/L + bleeding); Cryoprecipitate (if low fibrinogen); NO heparin in bleeding patient

data.symptoms: Petechiae, purpura, bruising, bleeding, isolated low platelet count
data.treatment_drugs: Prednisolone (1mg/kg/day for 2 weeks, then taper if response); IV immunoglobulin (1mg/kg/day for 3 days, works faster); splenectomy if fails

data.symptoms: Bleeding in neonate (vitamin K deficiency)
data.treatment_drugs: Vitamin K IM (prophylaxis); Fresh frozen plasma + Vitamin K (if active bleeding)

data.symptoms: Hypovolaemia, reduced skin turgor, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria
data.treatment_drugs: Sodium chloride 0.9% or Ringer's lactate (first litre over 15-20 mins, then maintenance)

data.symptoms: As above in child
data.treatment_drugs: Half-strength Darrow's with dextrose 2.5% (30ml/kg over first hour, then maintenance)

data.symptoms: Euvolemic patient unable to take oral fluids
data.treatment_drugs: 0.9% NaCl + 5% Dextrose + Potassium chloride (20mmol/L) – total 2-3 L/24hrs

data.symptoms: As above
data.treatment_drugs: Half-strength Darrow's with dextrose 2.5% (100ml/kg/24hrs for 0-10kg; 75ml/kg/24hrs for 5-10yrs)

data.symptoms: Up to 30 days old
data.treatment_drugs: Neonatalyte (up to 150ml/kg/24hrs, max 60ml/kg/24hrs on first day)

data.symptoms: Intraoperative fluid shift
data.treatment_drugs: Ringer's lactate (10ml/kg first hour, then 5ml/kg subsequent hours, max 3L adult)

data.symptoms: Temperature ≥38°C
data.treatment_drugs: Increase maintenance fluids by 5-10%

data.symptoms: Loss of gastric fluid via NGT
data.treatment_drugs: Replace ml-for-ml with 0.9% NaCl + Potassium chloride (13mmol/L)

data.symptoms: Airway obstruction, bronchospasm, hypotension, urticaria, flushing, angioedema, GI symptoms, cardiovascular collapse
data.treatment_drugs: Adrenaline 1:1000 IM (0.5-1ml adult, weight-based for children) – repeat every 10 mins; Oxygen 6L/min; IV fluids (0.9% NaCl or Ringer's lactate); Promethazine or Chlorpheniramine IV; Hydrocortisone IV; Nebulised Salbutamol for bronchospasm; Aminophylline IV if refractory

data.symptoms: Toxic ingestion/inhalation (rapid onset coma, seizures, hypotension, metabolic acidosis)
data.treatment_drugs: Sodium nitrite 3% IV + Sodium thiosulphate 50% IV

data.symptoms: Excessive bleeding due to heparin
data.treatment_drugs: Protamine 1% IV

data.symptoms: Ingestion of iron supplements, vomiting, diarrhoea, metabolic acidosis, shock, liver failure
data.treatment_drugs: Desferrioxamine po/iv

data.symptoms: Chronic exposure, abdominal pain, anaemia, neuropathy, encephalopathy
data.treatment_drugs: Dimercaprol IM + Calcium disodium edetate IM

data.symptoms: Acute or chronic exposure, neurological symptoms, renal toxicity
data.treatment_drugs: Dimercaprol IM or Penicillamine po

data.symptoms: Visual disturbances, metabolic acidosis, altered mental status, coma
data.treatment_drugs: Ethanol 50% diluted (1.5ml/kg orally then 0.5-1ml/kg every 2hrs for 4 days)

data.symptoms: Respiratory depression, pin-point pupils, coma, hypotension
data.treatment_drugs: Naloxone IV (0.01mg/kg, repeat as necessary)

data.symptoms: Acute dystonia, oculogyric crisis, muscle rigidity, torticollis
data.treatment_drugs: Biperiden IV/IM + Phenytoin slow IV
